00000002-0000-0000-c000-000000000000.name: Microsoft Azure AD Graph (legacy)
00000002-0000-0000-c000-000000000000.notes: The Azure AD Graph API (graph.windows.net) - legacy, deprecated in favor of Microsoft Graph. Appearing as a token audience implies use of legacy AAD Graph code paths.

00000002-0000-0ff1-ce00-000000000000.name: Office 365 Exchange Online
00000002-0000-0ff1-ce00-000000000000.notes: Exchange Online resource. Common audience on tokens scoped to Exchange/EWS.

00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000.name: Microsoft Graph
00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000.notes: The Microsoft Graph API itself - often appears as the audience (aud) of access tokens, not the client. Seeing this as appid is rare and worth investigating.

00000003-0000-0ff1-ce00-000000000000.name: Office 365 SharePoint Online
00000003-0000-0ff1-ce00-000000000000.notes: SharePoint Online resource. Common audience on tokens scoped to SharePoint.

04b07795-8ddb-461a-bbee-02f9e1bf7b46.name: Microsoft Azure CLI
04b07795-8ddb-461a-bbee-02f9e1bf7b46.notes: FOCI client. Public client, no client secret. Heavy abuse vector for token-theft scenarios; refresh tokens are family refresh tokens (FRTs) redeemable across all 11+ FOCI clients.

04f0c124-f2bc-4f59-8241-bf6df9866bbd.name: Visual Studio (modern)
04f0c124-f2bc-4f59-8241-bf6df9866bbd.notes: First-party modern Visual Studio app id. Observed in Storm-2372 / APT29 device-code phishing waves (2025).

1950a258-227b-4e31-a9cf-717495945fc2.name: Microsoft Azure PowerShell
1950a258-227b-4e31-a9cf-717495945fc2.notes: FOCI client. The az/Connect-AzAccount cmdlet client. Frequently abused in red-team and stealer scenarios.

1b730954-1685-4b74-9bfd-dac224a7b894.name: Azure Active Directory PowerShell
1b730954-1685-4b74-9bfd-dac224a7b894.notes: Legacy AzureAD module client id. Also used by AADInternals tooling. Abused in Storm-2372 device-code waves.

1fec8e78-bce4-4aaf-ab1b-5451cc387264.name: Microsoft Teams
1fec8e78-bce4-4aaf-ab1b-5451cc387264.notes: FOCI client. Teams desktop and mobile.

29d9ed98-a469-4536-ade2-f981bc1d605e.name: Microsoft Authentication Broker
29d9ed98-a469-4536-ade2-f981bc1d605e.notes: NOT on Secureworks' canonical FOCI list, but the standard Windows client for Entra device-join and Primary Refresh Token (PRT) issuance. The favourite vector for PRT phishing per Dirk-jan Mollema's research; UTA0355 and Storm-2372 both used MAB.

872cd9fa-d31f-45e0-9eab-6e460a02d1f1.name: Visual Studio (legacy)
872cd9fa-d31f-45e0-9eab-6e460a02d1f1.notes: FOCI client. Legacy Visual Studio client id; abused in Storm-2372 / APT29 device-code phishing waves (2025).

9bc3ab49-b65d-410a-85ad-de819febfddc.name: SharePoint Online Management Shell
9bc3ab49-b65d-410a-85ad-de819febfddc.notes: SPO PnP / Management Shell client. Abused by AADInternals tooling.

aebc6443-996d-45c2-90f0-388ff96faa56.name: Visual Studio Code
aebc6443-996d-45c2-90f0-388ff96faa56.notes: First-party VS Code app abused by Volexity-tracked UTA0352 cluster in 2025 OAuth phishing campaign against Ukraine-related NGOs.

d3590ed6-52b3-4102-aeff-aad2292ab01c.name: Microsoft Office
d3590ed6-52b3-4102-aeff-aad2292ab01c.notes: FOCI client. The Microsoft 365 / Office desktop client. Token-theft target: a stolen Office FRT redeems for Graph access tokens with broad pre-consented scopes.

fb78d390-0c51-40cd-8e17-fdbfab77341b.name: Microsoft Exchange REST API Based PowerShell
fb78d390-0c51-40cd-8e17-fdbfab77341b.notes: Exchange Online PowerShell V2/V3 module client. Common in admin operations; also abused in token-theft scenarios.

fdd7719f-d61e-4592-b501-793734eb8a0e.name: Azure VPN client
fdd7719f-d61e-4592-b501-793734eb8a0e.notes: Azure VPN client id; appears in some Storm-2372 IOC sets.
